title: achillea umbellata subsp. pauciloba
intro: Achillea umbellata subsp. pauciloba, also known as the few-flowered yerba, is a subspecies of the yerba plant (Achillea umbellata) in the sunflower family. This perennial plant is native to North America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Achillea umbellata subsp. pauciloba include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and alleviating menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
